skill.name: pdf-extract-experimental-materials
skill.description: Extract experimental materials and instrument information from PDFs (or PDF-derived text/Markdown) into three CSV tables; use when a paper/report contains sections like Materials and Methods, Key Resources Table, Reagents, Antibodies, Consumables, Software, Equipment, Instruments, or Reagent Preparation.
skill.summary: Parse scientific papers to extract reagents, instruments, and preparation methods into structured CSV tables.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 44 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 9 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings scan plain text descriptions and JSON audit data, not actual cryptographic code. The 33 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code block formatting and CSV examples, not shell injection. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings reference the legitimate GitHub source repository. This skill is a well-documented scientific PDF extraction tool with no confirmed security issues. Legacy audit confirms all gates PASSED, score 91/100, grade 'Production Ready'.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm - false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 9 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across the audit JSON, validate_skill.py, and SKILL.md. All are FALSE POSITIVES: the JSON file contains static quality scores and execution results (no crypto code), validate_skill.py line 12 is an argparse help string, and SKILL.md lines 3/20/129/142 are plain English documentation sentences. No cryptographic operations exist in any file.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution - false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 33 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick characters are markdown code block delimiters used for formatting bash command examples and CSV schema illustrations. No actual shell command execution or code injection occurs. The content includes CLI usage examples such as 'python scripts/validate_skill.py --help' and CSV column header examples.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 2 instances of 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md line 7. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The URL https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills is a legitimate source repository link used for attribution. It is not a data exfiltration endpoint or malicious network call. The skill documents its origin transparently.

content.user_title: Extract experimental materials from PDFs to CSV tables
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually cataloging reagents, instruments, and preparation methods from scientific papers. This skill automates the process by parsing PDF-derived text into three structured CSV tables with fixed schemas, following strict non-hallucination rules.
actual_capabilities: Parse Key Resources Tables and Materials and Methods sections from PDF-derived Markdown or text
Extract reagents, antibodies, consumables, and software versions into a normalized CSV table
Extract instrument and equipment details (name, brand, model) into a structured CSV table
Extract reagent preparation instructions (concentration, solvent, steps, incubation) into a CSV table
Deduplicate entries by primary name and preserve the most complete row for each item
Enforce non-hallucination rules by leaving unknown brand, model, catalog, and version fields blank
limitations: Requires PDF-derived Markdown or text as input; cannot directly parse binary PDF files
Cannot infer missing brand, model, catalog number, or version information when not stated in source
OCR support requires external dependencies (Tesseract, pytesseract) for scanned PDFs only
Limited to materials and methods content; does not extract results, statistics, or discussion sections

use_cases.title: Catalog reagents from a biomedical paper
use_cases.description: A researcher reads a paper on cancer cell signaling and needs a structured list of all antibodies, chemicals, and consumables used in the experiments. The skill extracts this data into a clean CSV table.

use_cases.title: Build an equipment inventory from methods sections
use_cases.description: A lab manager collecting instrument specifications across multiple papers needs to extract equipment names, brands, and models into a standardized format for procurement planning.

use_cases.title: Standardize reagent preparation protocols
use_cases.description: A graduate student reproducing an experiment needs to capture buffer compositions, solution concentrations, and incubation steps from the methods text into a reproducible preparation table.

prompt_templates.title: Extract all materials from a paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the experimental materials from this scientific paper. Output three CSV tables: main_reagents.csv with columns name, brand, catalog_number; main_instruments.csv with columns instrument, brand, model; and reagent_preparation.csv with columns reagent, preparation_method. Leave any unknown fields blank.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic extraction from a paper with materials and methods section

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Key Resources Table to CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: This paper contains a Key Resources Table. Parse it and convert its contents into three normalized CSV tables: main_reagents.csv (name, brand, catalog_number), main_instruments.csv (instrument, brand, model), and reagent_preparation.csv (reagent, preparation_method). Deduplicate entries by primary name and keep the most complete row.
prompt_templates.scenario: Table-first parsing when a structured resource table is present

prompt_templates.title: Extract software versions from methods text
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan the methods section of this paper for all software tools mentioned. For each software item, extract the name, vendor or organization (if stated), and version number (if stated). Output the results following the main_reagents.csv schema where name is the software name, brand is the vendor, and catalog_number is the version.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus on software and analysis pipeline versions

prompt_templates.title: Full structured extraction with quality checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse the attached paper and produce three CSV tables for experimental materials. Use a table-first approach: scan for Key Resources Table, then Materials and Methods tables, then prose sections. For each entry, preserve original casing and punctuation. Deduplicate by name. Leave blank any field not explicitly stated. After extraction, spot-check three items against the source to confirm correctness.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with table-first parsing and validation

output_examples.input: The paper lists 'Anti-CD3 antibody (clone OKT3, BioLegend, cat. 317302)' under reagents and 'Nikon Eclipse Ti2 confocal microscope' under equipment.
output_examples.output:
main_reagents.csv:
name,brand,catalog_number
Anti-CD3 antibody,BioLegend,317302

main_instruments.csv:
instrument,brand,model
confocal microscope,Nikon,Eclipse Ti2

reagent_preparation.csv:
reagent,preparation_method
No preparation methods found in source.

output_examples.input: The methods section describes 'RIPA buffer: 150 mM NaCl, 1% NP-40, 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, 0.1% SDS, 50 mM Tris pH 8.0' and mentions 'Western blot imaging was performed on a Bio-Rad ChemiDoc MP system'.
output_examples.output:
main_reagents.csv:
name,brand,catalog_number
RIPA buffer,,

main_instruments.csv:
instrument,brand,model
ChemiDoc MP system,Bio-Rad,

reagent_preparation.csv:
reagent,preparation_method
RIPA buffer,150 mM NaCl, 1% NP-40, 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, 0.1% SDS, 50 mM Tris pH 8.0

best_practices: Prefer PDF-derived Markdown as input over raw PDF text to preserve table structure for more accurate extraction
Always verify extracted data by spot-checking at least three entries against the original source document
Group multi-step reagent preparations into a single preparation_method entry with clear step separators
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for papers lacking a materials and methods section or key resources table
Do not fabricate brand names, catalog numbers, or model identifiers when the source does not state them
Do not attempt to extract experimental results, statistical analyses, or discussion content outside the materials scope

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts PDF-derived Markdown or plain text as input. It cannot process binary PDF files directly.

faq.question: What are the three CSV tables produced?
faq.answer: The skill outputs three CSV files: main_reagents.csv (name, brand, catalog_number), main_instruments.csv (instrument, brand, model), and reagent_preparation.csv (reagent, preparation_method).

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing information?
faq.answer: Unknown fields such as brand, model, catalog number, and version are left blank. The skill does not infer or fabricate missing data.

faq.question: Can this skill process scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Scanned PDFs require OCR with Tesseract and pytesseract. For text-based PDFs, pdfplumber is recommended.

faq.question: Does the skill extract software and version information?
faq.answer: Yes. Software names and versions from methods sections are captured in the main_reagents table, with name mapped to software name and catalog_number mapped to version.

faq.question: How does the skill handle duplicate entries?
faq.answer: Entries are deduplicated by primary name (reagent name or instrument name). The most complete row is kept for each unique entry.
